s1_l1_before: This policy applies to the website operating at the URL:
s1_l2: The service operator and personal data controller is: roundByte Sp.z o.o. with its registered office in Poznań.
s1_l3_before: The operator’s contact email address is:
s1_l4: The operator is the controller of your personal data with regard to data provided voluntarily in the Service.
s1_l5_intro: The Service uses personal data for the following purposes:
s1_l6_intro: The Service obtains information about users and their behaviour in the following ways:
s1_l6_o1: Through data voluntarily entered in forms, which are then stored in the operator’s systems.
s1_l6_o2: By storing cookie files on end-user devices.
s2_h: SELECTED DATA PROTECTION METHODS USED BY THE OPERATOR
s2_l1: Login and personal data entry areas are protected in the transport layer (SSL certificate). As a result, personal data and login credentials entered on the site are encrypted on the user’s computer and can only be read on the destination server.
s2_l2: Personal data stored in the database are encrypted in such a way that only the operator holding the key can read them. This protects the data if the database is stolen from the server.
s2_l3: User passwords are stored in hashed form. The hashing function is one-way — its output cannot be reversed, which is the current standard for storing user passwords.
s2_l4: The operator periodically changes its administrative passwords.
s2_l5: To protect data, the operator regularly performs security backups.
s2_l6: An important element of data protection is regular updating of all software used by the operator to process personal data, including regular updates of software components.
s3_h: YOUR RIGHTS AND FURTHER INFORMATION ON HOW DATA ARE USED
s3_l1_intro: In certain situations, the controller may transfer your personal data to other recipients if this is necessary to perform a contract with you or to fulfil obligations incumbent on the controller. This applies to the following categories of recipients:
s3_l1_u1: companies providing marketing services for the controller
s3_l2: Your personal data processed by the controller will not be kept longer than necessary to carry out the activities related to them as specified in separate provisions (e.g. accounting). With regard to marketing data, data will not be processed for longer than 3 years.
s3_l3_intro: You have the right to request from the controller:
s3_l4: You have the right to object, within the scope indicated in point 3.3(c), to processing of your personal data for the performance of tasks carried out in the legitimate interests of the controller, including profiling, unless the controller demonstrates compelling legitimate grounds for the processing which override your interests, rights and freedoms, in particular for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims.
s3_l5: You may lodge a complaint with the President of the Personal Data Protection Office (Prezes Urzędu Ochrony Danych Osobowych), ul. Stawki 2, 00-193 Warsaw, concerning the controller’s actions.
s3_l6: Providing personal data is voluntary but necessary to use the Service.
s3_l7: You may be subject to automated decision-making, including profiling, for the purpose of providing services under the concluded contract and for direct marketing carried out by the controller.
s3_l8: Personal data are not transferred to third countries within the meaning of data protection law. This means we do not send them outside the European Union.
s4_l1: The Service collects information voluntarily provided by the user, including personal data, where such data are provided.
s4_l2: The Service may store information about connection parameters (timestamp, IP address).
s4_l3: In some cases, the Service may store information that helps link data in the form to the email address of the user completing the form. In such cases, the user’s email address appears inside the URL of the page containing the form.
s4_l4: Data provided in a form are processed for the purpose resulting from the function of the specific form, e.g. to handle a service request, business contact, service registration, etc. Each time, the context and description of the form clearly state what it is for.
s5_l1: Information about users’ behaviour on the Service may be logged. This information is used to administer the Service.
s6_l1_before: The operator uses statistical analysis of traffic on the site via Google Analytics (Google Inc. based in the USA). The operator does not transfer personal data to the provider of this service, only anonymised information. The service relies on cookies on the user’s end device. Regarding preferences collected by Google’s advertising network, the user can view and edit information from cookies using the tool:
s6_l2: The operator uses remarketing techniques that allow advertising messages to be matched to the user’s behaviour on the site, which may create the impression that the user’s personal data are used for tracking; in practice, however, no personal data are transferred from the operator to advertising providers. A technological requirement for such activities is that cookie support is enabled.
s6_l3: The operator uses the Facebook pixel. This technology means that Facebook (Facebook Inc. based in the USA) knows that a person registered with Facebook uses the Service. In this case it relies on data for which Facebook itself is the controller; the operator does not transfer any additional personal data to Facebook. The service relies on cookies on the user’s end device.
s6_l4: The operator uses a solution that analyses user behaviour through heat maps and session recordings on the site. This information is anonymised before being sent to the service provider so that it does not know which natural person it relates to. In particular, typed passwords and other personal data are not recorded.
s7_l2: Cookies are IT data, in particular text files, stored on the Service user’s end device and intended for use with the Service’s web pages. Cookies usually contain the name of the website they come from, their storage time on the end device and a unique number.
s7_l3: The entity placing cookies on the Service user’s end device and accessing them is the operator of the Service.
s7_l4_intro: Cookies are used for the following purposes:
s7_l4_o1: maintaining the Service user’s session (after login) so that the user does not have to re-enter login and password on every subpage of the Service;
s7_l4_o2: purposes described above under “Key marketing techniques”;
s7_l5: The Service uses two main types of cookies: “session” cookies and “persistent” cookies. “Session” cookies are temporary files stored on the user’s end device until logout, leaving the website or closing the browser. “Persistent” cookies are stored on the user’s end device for the time specified in the cookie parameters or until deleted by the user.
s7_l6: Web browsing software (a browser) usually allows cookies to be stored on the user’s end device by default. Service users can change these settings. The browser allows cookies to be deleted. It is also possible to block cookies automatically. Detailed information is available in the browser’s help or documentation.
s7_l7: Restricting cookies may affect some features available on the Service’s web pages.
s7_l8: Cookies placed on the Service user’s end device may also be used by entities cooperating with the operator of the Service, in particular: Google (Google Inc. based in the USA), Facebook (Facebook Inc. based in the USA), Twitter (Twitter Inc. based in the USA).
